title: Reverse-over-NESTED-forward: gradient over derivative-of-derivative loses the d12 mixed coefficient's reverse-tape identity -> returns 0
goal: Downstream (Noesis) report, verified on master afd32a4c. reverse-over-SINGLE-forward works (#113): (gradient (lambda(p)(derivative (lambda(x)(* (vref p 0) x x)) 2.0)) #(3.0)) = #(4). But reverse-over-NESTED-forward returns #(0), expected #(2): (gradient (lambda(p)(derivative (lambda(x)(derivative (lambda(xx)(* (vref p 0) xx xx)) x)) 2.0)) #(3.0)). Math: f=p*x^2, d2/dx2=2p, d/dp=2. Root cause (consumer's precise diagnosis, matches #113 architecture): the nested derivative uses the 4-component jet {value, d1*e1, d2*e2, d12*e1*e2}; the SECOND derivative lives in d12. #113's eshkol_ad_mixed_record wires the d1/single-forward tangent onto the reverse tape connected to p, but the d12 mixed coefficient is extracted/packed as a plain numeric tangent, erasing its reverse-tape identity -> outer gradient sees a constant wrt p -> 0. Fix: extend the mixed-record mechanism so the d12 coefficient of a nested (2-level) forward derivative also stays an AD-visible tape node connected to the captured reverse variable.
detection_gap: The AD oracle (tests/ad_oracle/gen_ad_oracle.py) covers nest cells dofd/gofd/gofg but the reverse-outer composition only reaches nesting depth 1 (gofd = gradient-over-single-derivative). It never generates gofdofd (gradient over derivative-of-derivative). ADD that cell + generally reverse-outer x inner-nesting-depth-2, scalar AND vector param, so this class is permanently gated.
acceptance: the nested repro returns #(2) (-r AND AOT); single-forward still #(4); a 2-vector nested case matches finite-difference; AD oracle extended with gofdofd cells and 54->N/N green (0 xknown); no regression to nested_ad/mixed_mode/ad_input2/SICP
